author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
mudiba⋅madam udāraṁ mardayann aṅga⋅kāntyā
vasana⋅ruci⋅nirastāmbhoja⋅kiñjalka⋅śobhaḥ
taruṇima⋅taraṇīkṣāviklavad⋅bālya⋅candro
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

pitur aniśama⋅gaṇya⋅prāṇa⋅nirmanthanīyaḥ
kalita⋅tanur ivāddhā mātṛ⋅vātsalya⋅puñjaḥ
anuguṇa⋅guru⋅goṣṭhī⋅dṛṣṭi⋅pīyūṣa⋅vartir
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

akhila⋅jagati jāgran⋅mugdha⋅vaidagdhya⋅caryā⋅
prathama⋅gurur udagra⋅sthāma viśrāma⋅saudhaḥ
anupama⋅guṇa⋅rājī⋅rañjitāśeṣa⋅bandhur
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

api madana⋅parārdhair duṣkaraṁ vikriyormiṁ
juvatiṣu nidadhāno bhrū⋅dhanur dhūnanena
priya⋅sahacara⋅varga⋅prāṇa⋅mīnāmbu⋅rāśir
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

nayana⋅śṛṇi⋅vinoda⋅kṣobhitānaṅganāgon
mathita⋅gahana⋅rādhā⋅citta⋅kāsāra⋅garbhaḥ
praṇaya⋅rasa⋅marandāsvāda⋅līlā⋅ṣaḍ⋅aṅghrir
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

anupadam udayantyā rādhikāsaṅga⋅siddhyā
sthagita⋅pṛthur athāṅga⋅dvandva⋅rāgānubandhaḥ
madhurima⋅madhu⋅dhārā⋅dhoraṇīnām udanvān
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

alaghu⋅kuṭila⋅rādhā⋅dṛṣṭi⋅vārī⋅niruddha⋅
trijagad⋅aparatantroddāma⋅ceto⋅gajendraḥ
sukha⋅mukhara⋅viśākhā⋅narmaṇā smera⋅vaktro
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

tvayi rahasi milantyāṁ sambhrama⋅nyāsa⋅bhugnāpy
uṣasi sakhi tavālī⋅mekhalā paśya bhāti
iti vivṛta⋅rahasyair hrepayann eva rādhāṁ
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājaḥ kāṅkṣitaṁ me kṛṣīṣṭa

vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rājasyāṣṭakaṁ tuṣṭa⋅buddhiḥ
kalita⋅vara⋅vilāsaṁ jaḥ prayatnād adhīte
parijana⋅gaṇanāyāṁ nāma tasyānurajyan
vilikhati kila vṛndāraṇya⋅rājñī⋅rasajñaḥ
en_translation:
By the beauty of His limbs He crushes the pride of the god of love, and the splendor of His clothing surpasses the beauty of the lotus filament. He is the moon-like child whose immaturity is overshadowed by His rising youth; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

He is like the churning rod that churns the very life of His father, and He is like the personified affection of His mother, Yaśodā. He is the nectar-like vision for the assembly of elders and cowherd girls; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

He is the original teacher of captivating and skillful deeds, awakened in the entire world, and the resting place of supreme strength. He is the friend who delights everyone with His incomparable qualities; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

By the twitching of His bow-like eyebrows, He creates waves of agitation in young women that are difficult even for Cupid with his countless arrows. He is the ocean for the fish-like lives of His dear companions; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

He churns the deep lake of Rādhā's heart like a wild elephant, disturbed by the playful hooks of His eyes. He is the six-legged bee playing to taste the nectar of the mellow of divine love; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

With the success of His union with Rādhikā, which arises at every step, His body is bound by Their mutual affection. He is an ocean of continuous streams of the honey of sweetness; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

His great mind-elephant, which is unrestrained in the three worlds, is held captive by the abundant, crooked glances of Rādhā. With a smiling face due to the joyful jokes of Viśākhā, may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

"O friend, look! Even though it was crushed in the confusion of Your secret meeting, Your belt of friends shines in the morning!" Thus, by revealing these secrets, He makes Rādhā shy; may the youthful prince of Vraja fulfill my desires.

One who diligently studies these eight verses of the youthful prince of Vraja—which are full of excellent pastimes—will have his name recorded in the list of Her intimate associates by the Queen of Vṛndāvana, who is an expert in divine mellows.
